---
title: Recreation SDSFIE Crosswalk
---

**Status:** v2 review draft
**Date:** 2026-06-01
**Sources:** Recreation v2 artifacts; SDSFIE 5.0 Gold Draft vocabulary, ontology, and enumerations

## Purpose

This crosswalk records where SDSFIE terminology can be mapped cleanly to the recreation ontology without making SDSFIE's feature model govern the recreation conceptual model.

The recreation model keeps these boundaries:

- `rec:*` classes are business/domain concepts.
- `recs:*Feature` classes are GIS representation classes.
- `recv:*` concepts preserve recreation source vocabulary.
- `sd:*` and `sde:*` terms are SDSFIE alignment anchors.

## Mapping Semantics

Predicates are written from the recreation term to the SDSFIE anchor.

| Predicate | Meaning in this crosswalk |
| --- | --- |
| `skos:exactMatch` | The recreation term and SDSFIE anchor can be treated as equivalent for the mapped feature concept. |
| `skos:closeMatch` | The concepts are operationally close, but differ by model grain, feature/entity boundary, or implementation convention. |
| `skos:broadMatch` | The SDSFIE anchor is broader than the recreation term. |
| `skos:narrowMatch` | The SDSFIE anchor is narrower than the recreation term. |
| `skos:relatedMatch` | The terms are relevant to one another but should not be treated as a hierarchy or equivalence. |
| No mapping | No class-level SDSFIE anchor is clear enough to assert by default. |

## Spatial Feature Crosswalk

| Recreation term | Recreation meaning | SDSFIE anchor | Mapping | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `recs:RecreationAreaFeature` | GIS representation of `rec:RecreationArea` | `sd:RecreationBoundary` | `skos:closeMatch` | SDSFIE's boundary represents the overall recreation area. Recreation keeps the managed area entity separate from its boundary feature. |
| `recs:RecreationFacilityFeature` | Generic facility representation | `sd:RecreationFeature` | `skos:broadMatch` | SDSFIE `RecreationFeature` is a general storage class for recreation objects and assets. |
| `recs:RecreationAmenityFeature` | Generic amenity representation | `sd:RecreationFeature` | `skos:broadMatch` | Amenity is a clearer recreation role than the flat SDSFIE feature bucket. |
| `recs:CampgroundFeature` | Campground spatial representation | `sd:Campground` | `skos:exactMatch` | SDSFIE also allows the `campground` recreation feature type value when using the generic feature class. |
| `recs:CampsiteFeature` | Individual campsite representation | `sd:Campsite` | `skos:exactMatch` | SDSFIE campsite use values can be implementation attributes, not recreation OWL subclasses by default. |
| `recs:DayUseAreaFeature` | Managed day-use area representation | `sd:RecreationFeature` | `skos:broadMatch` | SDSFIE has no clean day-use-area class-level anchor. |
| `recs:PicnicAreaFeature` | Picnic area representation | `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_picnicSite` | `skos:closeMatch` | SDSFIE models this as a recreation feature type value rather than a standalone class. |
| `recs:MarinaFeature` | Managed boating facility representation | `sd:Marina` | `skos:closeMatch` | SDSFIE marina is a harbor facility. Recreation marina is a managed recreation facility and water access asset. |
| `recs:WaterAccessFeature` | Generic water access representation | `sd:RecreationFeature` | `skos:relatedMatch` | Water access is a recreation facet that can cut across several SDSFIE classes and values. |
| `recs:BoatRampFeature` | Recreational boat launch representation | `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_boatRamp` | `skos:closeMatch` | Do not map recreational boat ramps to `sd:BoatRamp`; SDSFIE scopes that feature class to non-recreational ramps. |
| `recs:FishingPierFeature` | Fishing platform or pier representation | `sd:FishingFeature` | `skos:broadMatch` | SDSFIE fishing feature is broader than a fishing pier. |
| `recs:FishingPierFeature` | Fishing platform or pier representation | `sd:DockOrWharf` | `skos:relatedMatch` | Useful when the source feature is also managed as dock or wharf infrastructure. |
| `recs:FuelDockFeature` | Marine fuel service asset representation | `sd:DockOrWharf` | `skos:broadMatch` | SDSFIE has the generic water-land interface structure; fuel service is the narrower recreation asset role. |
| `recs:SwimmingAreaFeature` | Designated swimming area representation | `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_swimmingDesignatedBeach` | `skos:closeMatch` | Close when the source feature is a designated beach. |
| `recs:SwimmingAreaFeature` | Designated swimming area representation | `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_waterActivitySite` | `skos:broadMatch` | Use for broader water activity site implementations. |
| `recs:TrailFeature` | Managed recreation route representation | `sd:RecreationTrail` | `skos:exactMatch` | Trail use values remain attributes or source concepts unless separate classes are justified. |
| `recs:ParkingAreaFeature` | Recreation parking area representation | `sd:ParkingArea` | `skos:exactMatch` | SDSFIE explicitly separates parking area mapping from pavement management segmentation. |
| `recs:RestroomFeature` | Restroom representation | `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_restroom` | `skos:closeMatch` | SDSFIE restroom value is flush-oriented. Vault toilets map separately. |
| `recs:VisitorCenterFeature` | Visitor information and interpretation facility representation | `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_museumOrVisitorCenter` | `skos:closeMatch` | SDSFIE combines museums and visitor centers under one feature type value. |
| `recs:LandClassificationAreaFeature` | Source-backed land classification area | `sd:LandUse` | `skos:relatedMatch` | SDSFIE land use is related, but not identical to USACE recreation land allocation or classification concepts. |
| `recs:WaterSurfaceZoneFeature` | Source-backed water surface zone | `sd:RestrictedArea` | `skos:relatedMatch` | Applies only when the zone restricts access or activity. Other water-surface zones require source-specific mapping. |
| `recs:ProjectBoundaryFeature` | Project boundary representation | No default mapping | No mapping | A project boundary is not automatically a SDSFIE recreation boundary. Map to `sd:RecreationBoundary` only when the source explicitly represents a recreation area boundary. |

## Domain Terms Without Current Spatial Feature Classes

These recreation domain classes have useful SDSFIE anchors, but the active spatial extension does not currently define separate `recs:*Feature` classes for each one.

| Recreation term | SDSFIE anchor | Mapping | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `rec:PicnicShelter` | `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_picnicShelter` | `skos:closeMatch` | SDSFIE models picnic shelter as a recreation feature type value. |
| `rec:Playground` | `sd:Playground` | `skos:closeMatch` | The SDSFIE class is spatial/entity oriented; recreation keeps playground as an amenity domain class. |
| `rec:BoatSlip` | `sd:DockOrWharf` | `skos:relatedMatch` | A slip is a managed mooring location, not necessarily the dock or wharf structure itself. |
| `rec:PumpOutStation` | `sd:DockOrWharf` | `skos:relatedMatch` | Related when the station is located on dock infrastructure; no default equivalence. |
| `rec:Restroom` | `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_restroom`; `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_vaultToilet` | `skos:narrowMatch` | SDSFIE separates narrower restroom feature type values below the broader recreation restroom domain class. |
| `rec:FlushRestroom` | `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_restroom` | `skos:exactMatch` | SDSFIE restroom definition is flush-water oriented. |
| `rec:VaultToilet` | `sde:RecreationFeatureUseType_vaultToilet` | `skos:exactMatch` | SDSFIE has a specific vault toilet recreation feature type value. |
| `rec:Trail` | `sde:RecreationTrailUseType_hike`; `sde:RecreationTrailUseType_bike`; `sde:RecreationTrailUseType_equestrianOrPack` | `skos:narrowMatch` | SDSFIE trail use values are narrower than the general managed trail class. |
| `rec:HikingTrail` | `sde:RecreationTrailUseType_hike` | `skos:closeMatch` | SDSFIE trail use value can type a `recs:TrailFeature` implementation. |
| `rec:BikeTrail` | `sde:RecreationTrailUseType_bike` | `skos:closeMatch` | SDSFIE trail use value can type a `recs:TrailFeature` implementation. |
| `rec:EquestrianTrail` | `sde:RecreationTrailUseType_equestrianOrPack` | `skos:closeMatch` | SDSFIE combines equestrian and pack trail use. |

## SDSFIE Anchors To Use Carefully

| SDSFIE term | Crosswalk decision |
| --- | --- |
| `sd:BoatRamp` | Do not use as the recreational boat ramp match. SDSFIE scopes the class to non-recreational boat ramps and directs recreational ramps to `sd:RecreationFeature` with the boat ramp type value. |
| `sd:RecreationFeature` | Use as a broad anchor for generic recreation assets, not as a replacement for `rec:RecreationFacility`, `rec:RecreationAmenity`, or `rec:WaterAccessAsset`. |
| `sd:RecreationBoundary` | Use for overall recreation area boundaries, not for every recreation use area or project boundary. |
| `sd:DockOrWharf` | Use for dock/wharf infrastructure. Do not collapse boat slips, fuel docks, pump-out stations, and fishing piers into this class unless the source feature grain supports that interpretation. |
| `sd:LandUse` | Related to recreation land classification, but not equivalent to USACE source classification vocabularies. |
| `sd:RestrictedArea` | Related to water-surface zones only when the zone restricts access or activity. |

## Implementation Rule

Use SDSFIE mappings as crosswalk assertions and migration aids. Do not make SDSFIE classes superclasses of recreation classes unless an implementation profile explicitly requires that inheritance.
